titlestmt.titleproper: Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna - Suriname collection.8492
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:43:19 UTC.

ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08492Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna - Suriname collection.8492Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
February 20152B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
religThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:10:53 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna - Suriname collection. 8492
1 folders. 1 folders.
Undated Photographs, printed material, publications Photographs and printed material relating to the synagogue. Collection material in English 7787155
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
February 2015
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, February 2015
Date modified:
RMC Staff, February 2015
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Photographs and printed material relating to the synagogue, published in Paramaribo, Surinam.
Cite As:
Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna - Suriname collection, #8492m, Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Jews -- Surinam.
7787155 <<<<<<< HEAD DocumentsDescription of the "Restoration of Jewish Savannah"90 - Folder 1Article from "De West Van Woensdag" (9 Maart 1966) titled "Restauratie van Joden Savanna, Hof van Justitie en Huis van Bewaring" ("Restoration of the Jewish Savanah…")90 - Folder 1Paper in Hebrew which appears to be about the synagogue90 - Folder 1"Plan to reconstruct the Synagogue Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna – Suriname" booklet90 - Folder 1
Scope and Contents
Contents: 1. Illustration of the elevation by direction 2. Breakdown of each section of the synagogue, focus on the outside 3. Breakdown of the synagogue's interior 4. Plan of the synagogue, cemetery marked 5. Location of the synagogue in relation to the Suriname River 6. Estimate of the Reconstruction of the synagogue "Beracha ve Shalon" at Joden Savanna 7. Photos – 1U copy 2 "remainder, North-elevation"; 1Y copy 2 "remainder, West-elevation"; 1Z copy 2 "detail of a tomb-stone"; four photos showing the details of a tombstone with Hebrew writing, two winged children, a skull and crossbones, and other imageryPhotographs"Gezicht op de Joden Savanne" – Naar een teekening uit den atlas behoorende bij het werk van M. D. Teenstra „De Landbouw in de kolonie Suriname", 1835 (– "View of the Jewish Savannah," taken from the work of M. D. Teenstra, "Agriculture in the colony of Suriname"); shows two houses on a hill, one figure seated on a logPhoto #1A90 - Folder 1"Paramaribo", Augusta Currel; illustration of the capital of Suriname, flat boats with two figures each, houses on hills in the distancePhoto #1B90 - Folder 1"De Joden-Savanne" ("The Jewish-Savannah"); "Synagogue" written in the corner, illustrartion with two boats, small houses and figures on the shorePhoto #1C90 - Folder 1unlabeled; uneven field with stakes driven into the ground leading to a house in the distance, trees (including palm trees) breach the horizonPhoto #1D90 - Folder 1unlabeled; three boats, each with figures, one with two sails, the others with oars; several hills with houses, some on stilts.Photo #1Eunlabeled; two figures gazing at another figure gesturing toward a child; an overhang of wood and some foliage is nestled among rocky slopes, trees behind.Photo #1F90 - Folder 1"View of the Settlement called the Jews' Savannah"; two figures conversing in shadow, one seated, one reclining; behind are boats on a river and houses on a hillPhoto #1GThree figures, two reclining and one holding spears;, beside a tree; a house to their left; other houses and a review beforePhoto #1H90 - Folder 1"Begraafplaats, Joden-Savanne-Suriname" "( "Cemetary, Jewish-Savannah-Suriname"); a map of the cemetary marking graves; several graves have their iconography illustratedPhoto #1I90 - Folder 1"Post Gelderland en Joden Savannah" ("Gelderland [Province] in the Jewish Savannah"); appears to be a more detailed version of 1BPhoto #1J90 - Folder 1"Joden Savannah" – Gezigt op de Sunagoge en Kerkhof Van de Zude van het Militair Cordonpad Gezien ("Jewish-Savannah"- Sight of the synogague and graveyard...); appears to be a more detailed version of 1DPhoto #1K90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; holy Ark of the synagogue with covered Torah scrolls, candles, and a chandalier; synogague has a sand floorPhoto #1L90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; same photo as 1L except with a man in the lower right (white shirt, black tie, yamaka)Photo #1M90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; wider view of the synagogue (benches, at least four chandaliers) , man (white shirt, yamaka) standing in the centerPhoto #1N90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close-up of chandalier in the synagoguePhoto #1O90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close-up of standing candle-holder in the synogaguePhoto #1Pfrom Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; similar to 1L but with Ark closedPhoto #1Q90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close up of chandalier in the synogaguePhoto #1R90 - Folder 1P.J. Benoit: Voyage à Surinam, Bruxelles; No. 43: Village habité par des Juifs sur la rivière de Surinam (Lithographie van Lauters) (P.J. Benoit: Trip to Surinam, Brussels; No. 43: village inhabited by the Jewish people on the Surinam river); small houses on hills, figures on the path, river in the distancePhoto #1S90 - Folder 1PP3: J26.PN4; Suriname. (other writing cannot be made out completely); plants growing over smooth surfacePhoto #1T90 - Folder 1Remainder, North-elevation. Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, steps overgrown with grassPhoto #1U90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, wall with holes and overgrown with grassPhoto #1V90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, wall collapsed with grass growing through the bricksPhoto #1W90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, steps overgrown with grassPhoto #1X90 - Folder 1Remainder, West-elevation. Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, close up of steps overgrown with grassPhoto #1Y90 - Folder 1Detail of a tomb-stone. Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname [stamp faded, but it appears to be the same as the other photos); carving of hand holding axe and cutting off tree, encircled with engraving of leavesPhoto #1Z90 - Folder 1 ======= DocumentsDescription of the "Restoration of Jewish Savannah"90 - Folder 1Article from "De West Van Woensdag" (9 Maart 1966) titled "Restauratie van Joden Savanna, Hof van Justitie en Huis van Bewaring" ("Restoration of the Jewish Savanah…")90 - Folder 1Paper in Hebrew which appears to be about the synagogue90 - Folder 1"Plan to reconstruct the Synagogue Beracha ve Shalom at Joden Savanna – Suriname" booklet90 - Folder 1
Scope and Contents
Contents: 1. Illustration of the elevation by direction 2. Breakdown of each section of the synagogue, focus on the outside 3. Breakdown of the synagogue's interior 4. Plan of the synagogue, cemetery marked 5. Location of the synagogue in relation to the Suriname River 6. Estimate of the Reconstruction of the synagogue "Beracha ve Shalon" at Joden Savanna 7. Photos – 1U copy 2 "remainder, North-elevation"; 1Y copy 2 "remainder, West-elevation"; 1Z copy 2 "detail of a tomb-stone"; four photos showing the details of a tombstone with Hebrew writing, two winged children, a skull and crossbones, and other imageryPhotographs"Gezicht op de Joden Savanne" – Naar een teekening uit den atlas behoorende bij het werk van M. D. Teenstra „De Landbouw in de kolonie Suriname", 1835 (– "View of the Jewish Savannah," taken from the work of M. D. Teenstra, "Agriculture in the colony of Suriname"); shows two houses on a hill, one figure seated on a logPhoto #1A90 - Folder 1"Paramaribo", Augusta Currel; illustration of the capital of Suriname, flat boats with two figures each, houses on hills in the distancePhoto #1B90 - Folder 1"De Joden-Savanne" ("The Jewish-Savannah"); "Synagogue" written in the corner, illustrartion with two boats, small houses and figures on the shorePhoto #1C90 - Folder 1unlabeled; uneven field with stakes driven into the ground leading to a house in the distance, trees (including palm trees) breach the horizonPhoto #1D90 - Folder 1unlabeled; three boats, each with figures, one with two sails, the others with oars; several hills with houses, some on stilts.Photo #1Eunlabeled; two figures gazing at another figure gesturing toward a child; an overhang of wood and some foliage is nestled among rocky slopes, trees behind.Photo #1F90 - Folder 1"View of the Settlement called the Jews' Savannah"; two figures conversing in shadow, one seated, one reclining; behind are boats on a river and houses on a hillPhoto #1GThree figures, two reclining and one holding spears;, beside a tree; a house to their left; other houses and a review beforePhoto #1H90 - Folder 1"Begraafplaats, Joden-Savanne-Suriname" "( "Cemetary, Jewish-Savannah-Suriname"); a map of the cemetary marking graves; several graves have their iconography illustratedPhoto #1I90 - Folder 1"Post Gelderland en Joden Savannah" ("Gelderland [Province] in the Jewish Savannah"); appears to be a more detailed version of 1BPhoto #1J90 - Folder 1"Joden Savannah" – Gezigt op de Sunagoge en Kerkhof Van de Zude van het Militair Cordonpad Gezien ("Jewish-Savannah"- Sight of the synogague and graveyard...); appears to be a more detailed version of 1DPhoto #1K90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; holy Ark of the synagogue with covered Torah scrolls, candles, and a chandalier; synogague has a sand floorPhoto #1L90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; same photo as 1L except with a man in the lower right (white shirt, black tie, yamaka)Photo #1M90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; wider view of the synagogue (benches, at least four chandaliers) , man (white shirt, yamaka) standing in the centerPhoto #1N90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close-up of chandalier in the synagoguePhoto #1O90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close-up of standing candle-holder in the synogaguePhoto #1Pfrom Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; similar to 1L but with Ark closedPhoto #1Q90 - Folder 1from Voorbetere foto's, Boschman's foto studio, Waaldijkstraat 8, Tel. 3718, no title; close up of chandalier in the synogaguePhoto #1R90 - Folder 1P.J. Benoit: Voyage à Surinam, Bruxelles; No. 43: Village habité par des Juifs sur la rivière de Surinam (Lithographie van Lauters) (P.J. Benoit: Trip to Surinam, Brussels; No. 43: village inhabited by the Jewish people on the Surinam river); small houses on hills, figures on the path, river in the distancePhoto #1S90 - Folder 1PP3: J26.PN4; Suriname. (other writing cannot be made out completely); plants growing over smooth surfacePhoto #1T90 - Folder 1Remainder, North-elevation. Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, steps overgrown with grassPhoto #1U90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, wall with holes and overgrown with grassPhoto #1V90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, wall collapsed with grass growing through the bricksPhoto #1W90 - Folder 1Bureau Voor Architectuur en Stedebouw Van Oerle en Schrama, Paramaribo – Suriname (Office of Architecture and Urban Planning...Paramaribo – Suriname); ruined building, steps overgrown with grassPhoto #1X90 - Folder 1Remainder, West-elevation.
